claim: Expert-rubric scoring was associated with higher reported inter-judge agreement than generic-rubric scoring.
scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together
decision_use: Supports testing the bundled skill-grounded rubric design; does not identify which component produced the association.

claim: Generic-rubric scoring had lower reported inter-judge agreement, providing the comparator for the reported agreement difference.
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause
decision_use: Supports the existence of an agreement difference while disconfirming a causal claim about expert authorship.

claim: Expert-rubric scoring had lower reported judge variance than generic-rubric scoring.
decision_use: Supports operational consistency testing but not adoption as a valid professional measure.

claim: Generic-rubric scoring had higher reported judge variance, providing the comparator for the reported variance difference.
decision_use: Supports the observed consistency contrast while disconfirming any inference from consistency alone to validity.

claim: Automated output grades had weak reported concordance with human judgments.
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality
decision_use: Disconfirms readiness for immediate adoption and requires independent expert validation in a pilot.

claim: Automated skill grades had weak reported concordance with human judgments.
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges
decision_use: Disconfirms current process-validity readiness and requires blinded human process annotation.

claim: The skill execution ablation found directional, heterogeneous effects in a small bundled comparison.
scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled
decision_use: Supports a controlled pilot rather than adoption or definitive rejection.

claim: One configured system had a large reported directional skill gain.
scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim
decision_use: Supports testing for heterogeneous benefit while disconfirming a stable causal adoption claim.

claim: Deployment occurred in a minority of no-skill runs, suggesting a possible completion difference without identifying its cause.
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity
decision_use: Supports measuring deployment as an outcome and controlling integration, order, and stochasticity.

claim: A substantial minority of pairwise-winning outputs did not meet the reported expert readiness threshold.
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
decision_use: Disconfirms treating relative wins as sufficient for readiness and requires an absolute expert-readiness gate.
